orchestrator.description: Reads core spec, generates derived work packages, coordinates the delivery pipeline, and tracks progress across all agents.

spec-writer.description: Drafts, refines, and extends specifications. Ensures specs are unambiguous, complete, and testable with unique requirement IDs.

implementer.description: Writes code from work packages only. Cannot read core spec. Implements exactly what the work package describes.

reviewer.description: Reviews code for correctness, quality, and alignment with both the spec and work package. Provides VERDICT: PASS or VERDICT: FAIL.

tester.description: Writes and runs tests from work packages only. Cannot read core spec. Verifies requirements listed in the work package.

validator.description: Final validation against the core spec and acceptance criteria. Compares output against every AC and produces a structured pass/fail report.

derive.description: Generate or update derived work packages from core spec
derive.template: Read specs/core.md and generate self-contained work packages in specs/derived/. Each WP must have a unique ID (WP-NNN), title, description, requirements, context, and acceptance criteria. Workers (implementer/tester) only see these WPs, never the core spec. If derived WPs already exist, diff against them and update only what changed. Preserve WP IDs for unchanged packages. $ARGUMENTS

implement.template: Read the work package at specs/derived/$1. Implement the code in the app/ directory following the requirements exactly. Do NOT read specs/core.md — you only have access to the work package. After implementation, report what files were created or modified.

pipeline.description: Run review → test → validate pipeline on recent changes
pipeline.template:
Run the quality pipeline on the app/ directory:

1. REVIEW: As the reviewer agent, review the code changes against specs/core.md and the relevant work packages. Check for correctness, quality, and spec alignment. End with VERDICT: PASS or VERDICT: FAIL.

2. TEST: As the tester agent, write and run tests for the implemented work packages. End with VERDICT: PASS or VERDICT: FAIL.

3. VALIDATE: As the validator agent, compare the implementation against every acceptance criterion in specs/core.md. Produce a structured report. End with VERDICT: PASS or VERDICT: FAIL.

Save results to .state/ directory as JSON. If any stage fails, report the failures clearly. $ARGUMENTS

bug-report.description: File a bug report in specs/bugs/
bug-report.template: Create a bug report in specs/bugs/ with the next available BUG-NNN ID. Include: title, description, steps to reproduce, expected vs actual behavior, related work package ID, and failing acceptance criteria. Bug: $ARGUMENTS

status.template:
Analyze the current state of the project:
1. Read specs/core.md and list all acceptance criteria (AC-01 through AC-17)
2. Check which work packages exist in specs/derived/
3. Check what code exists in app/src/
4. For each AC, determine if it is: NOT STARTED, IN PROGRESS, or DONE
5. List any bugs in specs/bugs/
6. Show a summary table of progress
